description: Machine-readable metadata for the experimental executable corpus used by the Hub batch testing workflow.

query_defaults.auto_triage_query: Analyze this executable at the program level. Identify the main behaviors, important functions, likely obfuscation or packing, and which available tools were most informative.
query_defaults.followup_query: Using the prior triage context, summarize what this sample does, explain the most relevant functions, and note any naming or type suggestions that would help future reverse-engineering.

samples.default_query: Analyze this executable and explain the core loop structure, key functions, and why it should be treated as a baseline decompilation sample.
Analyze this executable and explain how the string table is stored, accessed, and recovered.
Analyze this executable and explain the callback dispatch design, the handlers, and the underlying control flow.
Analyze this executable, explain the config decoding logic, and recover the most relevant decoded values.
Analyze this executable and explain the layered encoding, recovered strings, and the strongest behavioral conclusions.
Analyze this executable and explain the hash-based dispatcher, the likely algorithm, and the most relevant handlers.
Analyze this executable and explain the embedded payload format, decode path, and any staged extraction behavior.
Analyze this executable and explain the anti-analysis features, misleading names, and the strongest behavioral conclusions.
